intent: Fix desktop and mobile file drag-to-folder movement by aligning release/long-press hit targets with visible card surfaces.
assumptions: The shared handleDropOnFolder action layer and folder move API are already correct.
The failure happens before the shared action layer is called.
Desktop users can release on any visible part of a folder card.
Mobile users can long-press the file thumbnail center where hover-only preview controls exist in the DOM.
Mobile pointerup can be delivered outside the source card on some touch paths.
Native HTML draggable on touch browsers can preempt the custom long-press pointer path and surface only system haptic feedback.
risks: Moving desktop drop handlers could break folder drag start if the nested draggable thumb is changed.
Mobile long-press must still ignore menus, checkboxes, and normal visible controls.
Hover preview clicks must keep working on desktop.
Selected-item batch drag semantics must remain unchanged.
Desktop mouse drag/drop must continue using native HTML drag.
steps: Add failing tests for desktop full-folder-card drop and mobile hidden-preview long-press startup.
Add a failing test for mobile pointerup delivered outside the source file card.
Move folder card drop handling to the full data-folder-id card surface.
Mark hover-only preview action and keep its overlay non-interactive until desktop hover.
Register global mobile pointerup/pointercancel cleanup while a file long-press is pending or active.
Disable native HTML draggable on coarse pointers and suppress native contextmenu/callout while touch drag is pending.
Verify focused grid tests, drag-move action/list regressions, full frontend tests, lint, and build.
Add a permanent constraint and quality score entry.
